title: rabdosia henryi
intro: Rabdosia henryi is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Lamiaceae family, commonly known as mint or deadnettle. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of China and is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. Rabdosia henryi is known for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant's leaves and stems are typically used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, or dried extracts, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various ailments.
